hero.title: Turn one fixed template into a role-specific resume package
hero.description: Upload the resume you already have, extract structured content, rewrite it against a target JD, and export a polished Word or PDF without breaking the approved layout.

announcement.title: Built for fixed Word resume delivery, not free-form document editing

hero.tip: Designed for candidates, career coaches, and recruiting studios that need consistent output and faster turnaround.

introduce.title: A resume studio built around your template, not around a blank editor
introduce.label: Product overview
introduce.description: ResumeTailor keeps the source of truth in structured resume data. That makes extraction, editing, JD tailoring, and export much more stable than editing Word files directly in the browser.

items.title: Upload once
items.description: Accept the current resume file, then convert it into reusable structured sections.

items.title: Edit online
items.description: Fine-tune profile, experience, projects, skills, and links in a clean workspace.

items.title: Tailor to a JD
items.description: Paste the role title and JD to generate a stronger, job-specific version.

items.title: Export cleanly
items.description: Render everything back into your fixed Word template and keep PDF in sync.

benefits.title: Why this workflow is different from generic AI resume tools
benefits.label: Benefits
benefits.description: The product is opinionated: fixed templates, structured editing, guarded rewriting, and export-first delivery.

items.title: Template-first control
items.description: Users cannot upload arbitrary templates. Every tailored version lands in your approved layout, which keeps delivery quality and brand consistency stable.

items.title: Structured rewrite workflow
items.description: The system edits sections and bullets instead of guessing at a full Word document. That makes AI output easier to review, compare, and approve.

items.title: Delivery-ready exports
items.description: DOCX stays aligned to the template, PDF stays consistent for sharing, and both outputs come from the same structured source.

usage.label: Workflow
usage.title: From source resume to role-specific delivery in four steps
usage.description: The product workflow stays focused on extraction, review, tailoring, and export.

items.title: Upload the source resume
items.description: Bring the current resume into the workspace and bind it to the fixed template.

items.title: Extract structured sections
items.description: Split the file into profile, education, experience, projects, skills, and links.

items.title: Paste the target JD
items.description: Add the role title and full job description to guide the rewrite.

items.title: Review and export
items.description: Approve the final wording, then export DOCX or PDF from the same template.

features.title: What the first product version is optimized for
features.description: The initial release focuses on the fixed-template resume workflow that matters most.

items.title: Fixed template registry
items.description: Each template lives in a controlled registry and can be rolled out one by one.

items.title: Resume section schema
items.description: A consistent schema supports extraction, editing, AI rewriting, and export.

items.title: Role-specific tailoring
items.description: The product rewrites content according to the job title and JD instead of using generic prompts.

items.title: Fact-preserving guardrails
items.description: AI should improve phrasing and ordering, not invent achievements that are not present.

items.title: Version-ready workflow
items.description: The architecture is prepared for multiple tailored versions of the same base resume.

items.title: Export-oriented rendering
items.description: The final output is designed for clean delivery in Word and PDF.

stats.title: A narrow workflow by design
stats.description: The value comes from keeping the product focused on fixed-template resume delivery.

items.description: fixed template in the first release
core actions: extract, edit, tailor, export
delivery formats: Word and PDF

faq.title: Questions people will ask before using it
faq.description: These are the product rules that keep the experience stable.

items.question: Can users upload their own Word templates?
items.answer: No. The product is template-first. Users work inside your approved template set so exports remain consistent.

items.question: Does the AI rewrite invent new facts?
items.answer: It should not. The rewrite flow is designed to preserve facts and improve phrasing, ordering, and relevance to the target JD.

items.question: Why not edit the Word file directly in the browser?
items.answer: Direct Word editing is brittle for product workflows. Structured editing makes extraction, tailoring, review, and export much more reliable.

items.question: Will more templates be supported later?
items.answer: Yes. Additional fixed templates can be added into the template registry without changing the core product flow.

faq.tip: Need a studio workflow with multiple templates or review queues? The current information architecture already leaves room for that.

cta.title: Start building the fixed-template resume workflow
cta.description: The landing page and workspace are now aligned around the resume product. The next implementation steps are upload, structured parsing, JD tailoring, and export services.
